formulas.name: Try Block
formulas.notes: Used to enclose code that might throw an exception

formulas.name: Catch Block
formulas.variables: Exception type, code to handle exception
formulas.notes: Used to handle exceptions thrown by the try block

formulas.name: Throw Keyword
formulas.notes: Used to explicitly throw an exception

formulas.name: Finally Block
formulas.notes: Used to enclose code that must be executed regardless of exceptions

formulas.name: Exception Hierarchy
formulas.notes: Shows the hierarchy of exception classes in Java

formulas.name: Custom Exception
formulas.notes: Used to create custom exception classes

formulas.name: Throws Keyword
formulas.notes: Used to declare that a method may throw an exception

formulas.name: Multiple Catch Blocks
formulas.formula: try { // code } catch (Exception1 e) { // code } catch (Exception2 e) { // code }
formulas.variables: Exception types, code to handle exceptions
formulas.notes: Used to handle multiple types of exceptions

formulas.name: Nested Try-Catch Blocks
formulas.formula: try { try { // code } catch (Exception e) { // code } } catch (Exception e) { // code }
formulas.variables: Code to be executed, exception types, code to handle exceptions
formulas.notes: Used to handle exceptions within a try-catch block

formulas.name: Error Handling
formulas.notes: Error handling is the process of catching and handling errors that occur during the execution of a program
